perturbation.original_text: Within fifteen (15) days of any termination of this Agreement, Customer shall pay to eGain all unpaid fees accrued prior to termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Within thirty (30) days of any termination of this Agreement, Customer shall pay to eGain all unpaid fees accrued prior to termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement specifies that upon termination, the customer must pay all outstanding fees within fifteen (15) days. The modification extends this payment deadline to thirty (30) days. This in-text contradiction in section 10.2, makes it unclear what is the time period a customer has to pay the outstanding fees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Effect of Termination. Upon any termination of this Agreement, eGain shall immediately cease providing all Hosting Services, and Customer shall no longer have access to the Software or the eGain System. Except in the event of termination for Customer's breach, eGain shall provide Customer with an electronic copy of the final Reports (covering the month just prior to termination of this Agreement). eGain shall be entitled to retain a copy (whether electronic or otherwise) of the Online Messages and the Reports for its records and internal purposes and shall not disclose such Online Messages or Reports to any third party except as permitted under Section 4. Within thirty (30) days of any termination of this Agreement, Customer shall pay to eGain all unpaid fees accrued prior to termination. Sections 4, 5 (as to amounts accrued but unpaid), 7, 8, 10.2 and 12 and Exhibit A (as to amounts accrued but unpaid) shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the payment deadline from fifteen (15) days to thirty (30) days, which directly contradicts the original clause in the 'Contradicted Text' that states, 'Within thirty (30) days of any termination of this Agreement, Customer shall pay to eGain all unpaid fees accrued prior to termination.' The original version of the changed text had a 15 day window, and the modified one changes it to 30, which is now contradictory.
